overview: Build a Windows-first C# .NET child-process supervisor as a new project, implemented as a .NET worker that can run as a console app or Windows service. It should start configured managed processes and agent processes, tail stdout/stderr plus optional external log files, detect configured signal patterns, apply simple reactions, expose a minimal local HTTP API, and prioritize long-run stability plus real-time and retrospective debugging over broad feature scope.
openQuestions: Assume no database in the first version; keep state and recent event history in memory only.
Assume configuration is file-based JSON loaded at startup, with manual restart required after config changes in the first version.
Assume the local HTTP API binds to localhost only and does not include authentication in the first version.
Assume agent processes are supervised the same way as normal processes, with optional extra metadata but no separate orchestration protocol.
Assume signal matching uses plain substring and regex patterns with case-sensitivity configurable per rule.
Assume reactions are limited to kill, restart, priority up, priority down, start helper, stop helper, and internal state transition events.

stories.title: Create runnable DeerHunter worker host
stories.description: Set up a new .NET worker-based project that runs as a console app by default and is structured to support Windows service hosting, with minimal configuration loading and startup validation.
acceptanceCriteria: A fresh clone can run `dotnet build` successfully for the DeerHunter solution or project.
Running the app in console mode with a valid minimal config starts the host and logs that the supervisor is idle with zero managed processes.
The host is wired for Windows service execution without requiring a separate codebase or duplicate startup path.
Negative case: starting with a missing or invalid config file exits with a clear validation error instead of starting partially.

stories.title: Supervise configured child processes
stories.description: Implement the core supervisor loop that starts configured processes, tracks lifecycle state, captures stdout and stderr streams, and supports manual stop and restart operations for each managed process definition.
acceptanceCriteria: Given two configured sample commands, DeerHunter starts both and exposes their current lifecycle states in memory.
When a managed process exits unexpectedly, the supervisor records the exit and applies the configured restart behavior for that process.
Stdout and stderr lines from a child process are timestamped and attached to the correct process identity in the event stream.
Negative case: if a process executable path is invalid, DeerHunter records a failed start event for that process and continues supervising other configured processes.

stories.title: Tail external log files and unify event ingestion
stories.description: Add optional tailing of configured external log files and feed stdout, stderr, and tailed file lines through one normalized event pipeline so runtime behavior is easy to inspect and debug.
acceptanceCriteria: A configured external log file is tailed from the chosen starting position and new appended lines appear in the same normalized event stream as stdout and stderr entries.
Each ingested line includes source metadata that distinguishes stdout, stderr, and specific external log files.
If a tailed file is rotated or recreated in a normal Windows workflow, DeerHunter resumes tailing the active file without duplicating large historical content.
Negative case: if an optional external log file does not exist at startup, DeerHunter records a warning and continues running without crashing.

stories.title: Match signal patterns and execute reactions
stories.description: Support configurable signal rules that match normalized log events and trigger simple reactions including kill, restart, priority changes, helper start or stop, and internal state transitions.
acceptanceCriteria: A configured substring or regex rule can match an incoming event line and emit a recorded signal match event with the matched process, source, and rule identity.
On a configured restart reaction, DeerHunter stops and restarts the target process and records both the trigger reason and resulting lifecycle events.
On helper reactions, DeerHunter starts or stops the named helper process only when the referenced helper is defined in configuration.
Negative case: if a rule references an unknown target process or helper, DeerHunter records a rule execution failure and keeps the supervisor running.

stories.title: Expose local monitoring and manual control API
stories.description: Provide a minimal localhost-only HTTP API that returns current process states and recent events and allows manual actions such as start, stop, restart, and reaction-safe priority adjustments.
acceptanceCriteria: A local HTTP request can retrieve the current list of managed processes with stable identifiers, configured names, and current runtime states.
A local HTTP request can retrieve a recent bounded event history containing process lifecycle, log ingestion, signal match, and reaction events.
A local HTTP request can trigger a manual restart for a configured process and the resulting action appears in the event history.
Negative case: requests for an unknown process identifier return a clear client error response instead of affecting other processes.

stories.title: Harden for multi-day runs and debugging
stories.description: Add bounded in-memory retention, structured diagnostic logging, clear state transitions, and focused automated tests so DeerHunter remains stable over long runs and easy to debug in real time and retrospect.
acceptanceCriteria: Recent event retention is bounded by configuration or safe defaults so log-heavy workloads do not cause unbounded memory growth during long runs.
Structured supervisor logs make it possible to reconstruct process starts, exits, signal matches, reactions, and manual API actions after the fact.
Automated tests cover at least one restart path, one failed process start path, one signal reaction path, and one API not-found path.
Negative case: under repeated noisy log input, DeerHunter preserves service responsiveness and drops or trims only according to explicit bounded retention rules rather than failing unpredictably.
